commit.message:
feat: always-on voice, barge-in, session fixes & layout ## Voice UX (always-on, no push-to-talk) - Replace push-to-talk with always-on microphone that activates on connect - VAD-based barge-in: RMS > 600 threshold, 1s cooldown between interrupts - Track ARIA-speaking state via 600ms debounce; send {interrupted:true} to server - audioPlayer.bargeIn() stops all queued audio and resets playback schedule - Server already handled interrupt messages; client now correctly triggers them ## VoiceTab UI redesign - Animated orb: pulsing green = listening, blue ripple = ARIA speaking, spin = connecting - Chat bubble layout: user bubbles right/blue, ARIA bubbles left/dark - Waveform canvas strip showing live audio activity - Status bar shows mic/speaking state; no push-to-talk button - Newline characters in ARIA responses rendered as separate

bubbles - Transcript auto-scrolls to bottom on new messages ## Layout fix (header no longer scrolls off-screen) - Add fitHeight + className='app-tabs' to Cloudscape in App.jsx - Only .voice-messages scrolls; header bar + tab strip always stay visible ## Nova Sonic session reliability - Fix critical bug: is_active never set True — all while-loops exited immediately causing instant WS disconnect after connect - Transient errors (System instability detected, throttling, 600s limit) now trigger silent stream renewal instead of terminating the session - _session_watchdog renews at 560s completely silently — no user-visible messages - History injected as AWS-style role-tagged content blocks on renewal ## Auth stale-closure fix - configRef = useRef(config) + useEffect to keep ref current - ws.onopen reads configRef.current (always latest) not closure-captured config - Ensures authenticated=true and customer_id reach Nova Sonic on session start ## File logging - _configure_logging() in agentcore_app.py: RotatingFileHandler (aria_agentcore.log, 10MB x5) + StreamHandler; called at import so all aria.* loggers get handlers - All exception handlers in run() log with exc_info=True Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[paper] Write full content for Paper 7 results, validation, formulation, and discussion sections (#193)

Results section:
- Forward model predictions for 4 ripeness stages (f2=203.2 Hz unripe to 46.4 Hz overripe)
- f2^2 linearity verification (alpha=161.5 Hz^2/MPa, CoV=0.07%)
- Inversion roundtrip validation (<0.01% error)
- Sobol sensitivity analysis (E dominates with ST=0.54, then a with ST=0.38)
- Universal ripeness curve (Pi_ripe=0.062, CoV=3.3% across 4 cultivars)
- Multi-cultivar comparison (Crimson Sweet, Sugar Baby, Charleston Gray, Seedless)
- Parametric ripening sweep (239 Hz to 36 Hz over 20 stages)

Validation section:
- Yamamoto et al. (1980) watermelon comparison table
- De Belie et al. (2000) and Chen & De Baerdemaeker (1993) cross-species
- Monte Carlo geometric uncertainty (CoV=13.5% for ±10% geometry error)
- Individual parameter sensitivity analysis

Formulation section:
- Canonical parameter table
- Explicit stiffness decomposition (bending + membrane + pre-stress)
- Alpha=161.5 Hz^2/MPa derivation
- Explicit inversion formula with round-trip error bound
- Pi_ripe redefined to match code implementation

Discussion section:
- Quantitative ripening physics (factor 4.4 frequency drop)
- Biomedical FSI connection with specific numbers
- Updated limitations with Sobol-backed uncertainty bounds
- Practical implications with MC uncertainty quantification
- New generality subsection

All values computed from actual model runs, not estimated.

v5.10.0 (#102)

* ## v5.10.0
- *Enhancement:* `TestSharedState` updated to enable request-based (isolated) state; with the corresponding `GetHttpRequestId()` method now made public.
- *Enhancement:* Added `ApiTesterBase.AddPreRunAction()`, `AddPostRunBeforeExpectationsAction()`, `AddPostRunAfterExpectationsAction()` and `AddPostRunAction()` to enable the addition of actions to be executed at different stages of the `Run`/`RunAsync` process for every underlying test. - *Enhancement:* `TEntryPoint` methods can now be specified as `static`.

- *Fixed:* Reset `MockHttpClientRequest` internal state when using any `With*` method to ensure correct behavior.

[paper] Fix watermelon E_rind values: 25x increase to match literature tap-tone frequencies (#188)

Previous E_rind values (0.8-8 MPa) produced f2 = 11-41 Hz, far below the
published range of 60-250 Hz. Watermelon rind is cellulose-rich structural
tissue with E = 15-200 MPa.

Updated canonical parameters:
  unripe:   E = 200 MPa -> f2 = 203 Hz (lit: 140-250 Hz)
  turning:  E = 120 MPa -> f2 = 147 Hz (lit: 100-180 Hz)
  ripe:     E =  50 MPa -> f2 =  90 Hz (lit:  80-160 Hz)
  overripe: E =  15 MPa -> f2 =  46 Hz (lit:  40-80  Hz)

- ripeness_from_modulus() thresholds (25/80/160 MPa boundaries)
- parametric_ripening_sweep() E range (250-10 MPa)

All 333 tests pass.

commit.message:
fix: IAM propagation delay on CodeBuild project creation

When SDK deletes and recreates the CodeBuild execution role between
failed runs, CreateProject immediately fails because IAM takes 10-30s
to propagate trust policies.

Fixes:
- patch_agentcore_yaml(): verify each stored ARN actually exists in AWS
  via 'aws iam get-role' / 'aws ecr describe-repositories' before trusting
  it. Stale ARNs (role was deleted) are set to null so SDK auto-creates
  fresh resources instead of using a ghost reference.
- launch_agentcore(): retry loop (3 attempts, 30s wait between) that
  detects IAM trust policy errors in the output and retries. Also
  regenerates the YAML before each retry (SDK deletes it on failure).
- State file: clear stale CodeBuild/ECR ARNs from previous failed run.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: self-healing YAML regeneration + restore deleted config

The SDK deletes .bedrock_agentcore.yaml on failed runs, breaking
subsequent deploys with FileNotFoundError.

Fix: rewrite patch_agentcore_yaml() to always regenerate the YAML
from scratch using Python yaml.dump, pulling values from:
  1. State file (scripts/.deploy-state.json) — populated by deploy
  2. Existing YAML (if present) — preserves SDK-populated ARNs
  3. Script variables (AGENTCORE_REGION, ACCOUNT_ID, AGENT_NAME)

This means the YAML is always correct at deploy time regardless of
whether the SDK deleted or corrupted it in a previous run.

Also:
- Store SDK-populated values in state file (execution_role_arn,
  ecr_repository, codebuild_project_name, etc.) so regeneration
  works even on a fresh checkout
- Restore .bedrock_agentcore.yaml with all correct resource ARNs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: disable X-Ray observability on memory creation

enable_observability=False — X-Ray requires CloudWatch Logs as the
trace segment destination (UpdateTraceSegmentDestination) before it
can be enabled on a memory resource. We already have a full audit
pipeline (EventBridge → DynamoDB/CloudTrail/Firehose), so this SDK
observability flag is not needed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: memory creation - temp file for ID, spinner, safe YAML write

Root cause: Rich SDK console writes progress to stdout; $() capture
grabbed all of it; _patch_yaml_memory_id then wrote garbage into YAML.

Fixes:
- create_agentcore_memory(): write Python script to temp file (avoids
  heredoc stdin conflicts in background subshell); run in background
  with all output redirected to log file; write ONLY memory ID to a
  separate temp file; read temp file after bg process completes
- Rich Console forced to stderr=True so stdout is always clean
- Added spinner (⠋⠙⠹...) with elapsed time counter so user knows
  the script is alive during the 1-3 min provisioning wait
- _patch_yaml_memory_id(): use yaml.safe_load/yaml.dump instead of
  regex string manipulation — safe for any value content
- _patch_yaml_memory_mode(): new helper using yaml library
- State file and YAML restored with correct memory_id aria_bank_mem-sRaj7gH5rN
  (on next run the 'reuse existing' path will be taken instantly)
